sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citations resolved to accessible pages. The LOW_CONFIDENCE flag is nevertheless substantive because six cited claims were not established by their linked pages.

figures_match_sources.note: Only inputs.current_adoption_pct.url matched its quoted figure. The historical-analog, owner-age, consolidator-count, buy-multiple, exit-multiple, and pricing-mix citations do not establish the figures attributed to them.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The bounded role fractions are arithmetically coherent, but pi_dist=0.55 and the dominant-TEM role assumptions rely on an unverified 40-50% contingency revenue mix and an unestablished claim that TEM is the dominant 541618 subsegment. t50=3.5 is also derived partly from an unsupported historical analog.

rubric_consistent.note: The record uses unsupported research figures as scored inputs instead of marking them ESTIMATE with a complete auditable basis. BORDERLINE: |S - conditional cut 4.00| = 0.0396 is therefore unresolved and score-affecting. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S is within the orchestrator's stated tolerance and is not independently disqualifying.

cross_checks_honest.note: The terminal and no-proxy discussions are candid, but the pricing-model-deflation cross-check repeats an unsupported 40-50%/30-40% billing mix, and the adoption derivation describes an unsupported historical-analog claim as evidence.

source_audits.note: The BCM One vendor article lists TEM providers and their capabilities, but it does not document Calero's stated MDSL/Mobi acquisition dates or count 2-4 PE-backed platforms acquiring small 541618 consultancies in the last 24 months.

The page reports 5-7x EBITDA for $1M-$3M generalist consulting and 7-9x for specialty consulting, while 3.5-5.0x is an SDE range for specialty micro firms. It does not support the quoted 3-5x EBITDA entry band or 4.5x normalized EBITDA midpoint.

The Federal Reserve page reports about 33% BTOS AI adoption for professional, scientific, and technical services, while cautioning that the sector is eclectic and does not permit sub-sector heterogeneity analysis. It supports the broad-sector value, not a 541618-specific rate.

The accessible Capstone page reports 9.8x average EV/EBITDA across 2025 middle-market deals and 6.8x/9.8x expected typical/premium multiples. It does not report the quoted GF Data business-services median of 7.4x, consulting entry-to-exit expansion, or an 8.0x TEM platform exit multiple.

The page supports a 47% (10.6 percentage-point) methodology break for professional services, not a historical 7-10-year cloud/SaaS path to 50% adoption or a 36% rise in AI-driven TEM adoption in 2024.

Gallup reports that 52.3% of employer businesses are owned by people age 55+ and documents succession-plan gaps, including 27% of employers closing or unsure before retirement. It does not report that age-60+ owners are 37%, nor the quoted age-65 share.

SSI confirms its own parcel/freight audit service charges a contingency fee based on recoverable refunds. It does not concern the TEM market broadly and gives none of the claimed 42.6% managed-services share, 40-50% contingency mix, or 65% cloud-native implementation share.

reasons: inputs.buy_mult at https://ctacquisitions.com/prepare-your-business-for-sale/consulting-firm-exit/ uses 4.5x EBITDA, but the linked page's $1M-$3M EBITDA ranges begin at 5x for generalist and 7x for specialty consulting; rerun with a directly sourced, size- and industry-comparable normalized EBITDA transaction benchmark or clearly mark and justify an ESTIMATE.

inputs.exit_mult at https://www.capstonepartners.com/insights/report-capstone-partners-middle-market-mergers-and-acquisitions-valuations-index/ attributes a 7.4x business-services median and an 8.0x consulting-platform exit value to a page that reports different aggregate figures; rerun using a source that directly establishes a recent $10M+ EBITDA comparable for the scored segment.

inputs.active_consolidators at https://www.bcmone.com/blog/top-telecom-expense-management-companies/ asserts three active PE-backed add-on platforms without identifying or evidencing them; rerun with named platforms and dated add-ons in the last 24 months, or use an auditable ESTIMATE chain.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct at https://news.gallup.com/poll/657362/small-business-owners-lack-succession-plan.aspx uses 0.37, which the linked Gallup article does not report; rerun with an age-60+ statistic matching the input's population and unit, while separately retaining only the succession finding Gallup actually supports.

inputs.historical_analogs at https://www.federalreserve.gov/econres/notes/feds-notes/monitoring-ai-adoption-in-the-u-s-economy-20260403.html and inputs.pricing_structure at https://ssui.com/contingency-parcel-freight-invoice-audits/ contain unsupported adoption-history and billing-mix percentages that feed t50 and pi_dist; rerun with direct evidence for those exact figures or mark them ESTIMATE and provide a complete sourced derivation.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S
